paper_title: Analytical Methods for Pb-210 and Po-210 Analysis

methods.description: Process for preparing samples by wet-ashing with concentrated HNO3 and H2O2 to prevent evaporation of volatile polonium and convert residue to chloride form.

steps.description: Weighted sample was carefully wet-ashed in a beaker with concentrated HNO3 and 30% H2O2 completely.
steps.searchable_summary: wet-ash sample with HNO3 and H2O2

steps.description: Concentrated HCl was added after wet-ashing and the sample was continuously heated until nitrous fumes cease to be evolved.
steps.searchable_summary: convert residue to chloride form with HCl

steps.description: The solution was neutralized with saturated NaOH solution.

methods.method_name: Plating and Measurement of Alpha Activity
methods.description: Procedure for plating Po-210 on a silver plate and measuring alpha activity using an alpha scintillation counter.

steps.description: The solution was diluted to 200 ml with 0.5 M HCl solution and 100 mg of ascorbic acid was added.
steps.searchable_summary: dilute solution and add ascorbic acid

steps.description: Solution was transferred to a plating cell and placed in a water bath at 90-100°C, stirred for 4 hours.
The cell was dismantled, and the silver plate was rinsed with distilled water and dried at room temperature.
The silver plate was measured for the alpha activity by an alpha scintillation counter with ZnS detector.
steps.searchable_summary: measure alpha activity with scintillation counter

methods.description: Calculation of Pb-210 concentration in the sample using Po-210 activity measurements at different times.

steps.description: The concentration of Pb-210 at the time of sampling can be calculated from the amount present in a given sample at two later times t1 and t2 using the provided equation.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Pb-210 concentration using decay equation
